prompt: Marco ____ molto stanco.  (Marco is very tired.)
explanation: “è” (with accent) means “is”; “e” without the accent means “and”.

prompt: Vorrei pane ____ acqua.  (I'd like bread and water.)
explanation: “e” (no accent) means “and”; “è” with the accent means “is”.

prompt: Ogni giorno lui mi ____ una mano.  (Every day he gives me a hand.)
explanation: “dà” (with accent) means “gives”; “da” without the accent means “from/by”.
“da” (no accent) means “from/by”; “dà” with the accent means “gives”.

prompt: Il libro è ____, sul tavolo.  (The book is there, on the table.)
explanation: “là” (with accent) means “there”; “la” without the accent means “the/her (f.)”.

prompt: Ho visto ____ tua amica.  (I saw your friend.)
explanation: “la” (no accent) means “the/her (f.)”; “là” with the accent means “there”.

prompt: Aspettami ____ davanti alla scuola.  (Wait for me there in front of the school.)
explanation: “lì” (with accent) means “there”; “li” without the accent means “them (m.)”.

prompt: I biglietti? ____ ho comprati ieri.  (The tickets? I bought them yesterday.)
explanation: “li” (no accent) means “them (m.)”; “lì” with the accent means “there”.

prompt: Non voglio ____ pane ___ pasta.  (I want neither bread nor pasta.)
explanation: “né” (with accent) means “neither/nor”; “ne” without the accent means “of it/them”.

prompt: Torte? ____ ho fatte due.  (Cakes? I made two of them.)
explanation: “ne” (no accent) means “of it/them”; “né” with the accent means “neither/nor”.

prompt: Pensa solo a ____ stesso.  (He thinks only of himself.)
explanation: “sé” (with accent) means “himself/oneself”; “se” without the accent means “if”.

prompt: ____ piove, resto a casa.  (If it rains, I stay home.)
explanation: “se” (no accent) means “if”; “sé” with the accent means “himself/oneself”.
“sì” (with accent) means “yes”; “si” without the accent means “oneself/one (impersonal)”.

prompt: Qui ____ mangia bene.  (One eats well here.)
explanation: “si” (no accent) means “oneself/one (impersonal)”; “sì” with the accent means “yes”.

prompt: Bevo un ____ caldo.  (I drink a hot tea.)
explanation: “tè” (with accent) means “tea”; “te” without the accent means “you (stressed)”.
“te” (no accent) means “you (stressed)”; “tè” with the accent means “tea”.

prompt: Mio ____ lavora in banca.  (My dad works in a bank.)
explanation: “papà” (with accent) means “dad”; “papa” without the accent means “pope”.

prompt: Il ____ vive in Vaticano.  (The pope lives in the Vatican.)
explanation: “papa” (no accent) means “pope”; “papà” with the accent means “dad”.

prompt: Dammi la ____ della torta.  (Give me half of the cake.)
explanation: “metà” (with accent) means “half”; “meta” without the accent means “destination/goal”.

prompt: Roma è la nostra ____.  (Rome is our destination.)
explanation: “meta” (no accent) means “destination/goal”; “metà” with the accent means “half”.

prompt: Hanno aperto un nuovo ____ a Venezia.  (They opened a new casino in Venice.)
explanation: “casinò” (with accent) means “casino”; “casino” without the accent means “mess/brothel (colloq.)”.

prompt: In quella stanza c'era un gran ____.  (There was a real mess in that room.)
explanation: “casino” (no accent) means “mess/brothel (colloq.)”; “casinò” with the accent means “casino”.

prompt: ____ che dici è vero.  (What you say is true.)
explanation: “ciò” (with accent) means “that/this thing”; “cio” without the accent means “(non-word alone)”.

prompt: Un ____ lontano, tornerò.  (One distant day, I will return.)
explanation: “dì” (with accent) means “day (literary)”; “di” without the accent means “of”.
“di” (no accent) means “of”; “dì” with the accent means “day (literary)”.

prompt: L'anno scorso Dante le ____ a lungo.  (Last year Dante spoke to her at length.)
explanation: “parlò” (accented) is the past/future form of parlare; “parlo” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: Quella sera Maria lo ____ senza parlare.  (That evening Maria looked at him without speaking.)
explanation: “guardò” (accented) is the past/future form of guardare; “guardo” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: Nel 1945 mio nonno ____ dalla guerra.  (In 1945 my grandfather returned from the war.)
explanation: “tornò” (accented) is the past/future form of tornare; “torno” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: Nessuno le ____ quando lo disse.  (Nobody believed her when she said it.)
explanation: “credé” (accented) is the past/future form of credere; “crede” (no accent) is the present tense.
“partì” (accented) is the past/future form of partire; “parti” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: Domani ____ una bella giornata.  (Tomorrow it will be a nice day.)
explanation: “sarà” (accented) is the past/future form of essere; “sara” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: L'anno prossimo ____ trent'anni.  (Next year I will be thirty.)
explanation: “avrò” (accented) is the past/future form of avere; “avro” (no accent) is the present tense.

prompt: Chissà che tempo ____ domani.  (Who knows what the weather will be like tomorrow.)
explanation: “farà” (accented) is the past/future form of fare; “fara” (no accent) is the present tense.
